patterns_recognized: Negative-edge GOLD picks fail consistently: CHC@TEX (-4.41pp, 0-6), NYY@MIL (-2.00pp, 3-4) on 05-09 both lost; market holds SP-day form the model lacks. Edge must be positive (> 0pp) to sustain GOLD direction.

F3_swing_take_gap > 1000 cannot override an elite opposing SP: 05-10 ATL@LAD (F3=1774.2, LAD lost 7-2) proved F3 measures lineup contact quality, not total-game quality when opponent pitching dominates.

Stacked pipeline flags (acute_roster=True + confidence_downgrade=True) = reliable auto-DOWNGRADE: validated DET@KC 05-09, MIN@CLE 05-10 both would have been GOLD losses; the protocol has saved 3 of 3 applications.

PLATINUM tier historical hit rate ~43% (worse than GOLD); extreme model probabilities (> 0.85) fall into calibration bins with n=2 samples — statistically meaningless. Treat as model artifact, not genuine confidence.
Confirmed GOLD picks went 0-for-4 over 05-09 and 05-10 combined; the bar for GOLD CONFIRM needs two independent positive signals (F3 > 500 AND positive edge_pp AND no Stage 1/2 gap > 15pp).

NYY @ BAL.reasoning: Edge is -5.48pp: market prices NYY at 59.9% while model has them at 54.5% — the market is MORE bullish on NYY, so there is no value in backing them at the model price. Per 05-09 pattern, negative-edge GOLD picks failed 0-for-2 (CHC@TEX -4.41pp, NYY@MIL -2.00pp). F2_xwoba_gap=0.028 fires but one F-signal on a negative-edge pick has consistently proven insufficient. BAL SP Brandon Young is likely limited-experience, which may understate BAL's SP quality in the model. Downgrading to SKIP.
lessons_applied: single F-signal insufficient on negative edge

TB @ TOR.reasoning: Edge +6.75pp with F3_swing_take_gap=1224 (strong) and TB's outstanding 26-13 record. Live data confirms Kevin Gausman starts for TOR — a legitimate ace who can neutralize lineup quality advantage. Calibration maps model's 51.9% to ~47.8% actual rate, but market has TB at only 45.2%, so calibrated edge remains positive (~+2.6pp). No stress flags. f5_full_delta=0.05 (minimal Stage 1/2 gap). Gausman's elite quality is the key risk; TB's season-long quality and positive edge justify a CONFIRM at reduced confidence.
lessons_applied: elite opposing SP is risk factor per 05-10 lesson
calibration table applied to probability bucket

ARI @ TEX.reasoning: SKIP tier correctly applied — no F-signals fired despite positive edge of +9.17pp. Live data confirms both SPs are experienced veterans: Michael Soroka (ARI) vs Nathan Eovaldi (TEX). Near-.500 teams on both sides (ARI 19-20, TEX 19-21). Model has ARI winning F5 and full game (54.3%) while market has ARI at 45.2%. Without an active F-signal to confirm the directional edge, this remains a SKIP. Prior TEX home shutout streak (05-09, 05-10) was vs CHC specifically and does not generalize.
lessons_applied: experienced SP matchup reduces SP-edge uncertainty

SEA @ HOU.reasoning: Model (HOU 58.5%) and market (SEA ~57.8% implied) point in diametrically opposite directions. Live data confirms George Kirby starts for SEA — an elite contact-suppressor — vs HOU's Peter Lambert. F3=1783.5 is virtually identical to 05-10 ATL@LAD's F3=1774.2, which failed 7-2 when facing quality pitching. HOU is 16-25 (worst home-team record today) with acute_roster=True + confidence_downgrade=True stacked. Edge +16.34pp outside [4,15]pp band signals the market has substantially more information. Kirby's confirmed elite status and HOU's poor record override the model's F3 signal.
lessons_applied: F3 > 1000 fails vs elite SP (05-10 ATL@LAD pattern)
acute_roster + confidence_downgrade = auto-DOWNGRADE validated 3 consecutive days
market-model opposition on a losing home team favors market

SF @ LAD.reasoning: Three converging red flags: (1) PLATINUM tier has ~43% historical hit rate — automatic suspicion. (2) Model prob 0.9401 falls in calibration bin [0.9, 1.0] with only n=2 samples; this estimate is statistically meaningless and almost certainly a model artifact. (3) f5_full_delta=0.4633 (LAD F5 only 52.3% with Sasaki; full-game 94%) indicates the extreme full-game probability is driven by an artifact, not genuine late-inning thesis. Live data: SF SP Trevor McDonald (id=686790, likely recent debut) may have inflated F3=10190 via thin Statcast sample per heuristic-3. LAD direction is correct (Sasaki, 24-16 record), but 94% confidence is unjustified. Market at 65% is far more reasonable.
lessons_applied: calibration bin n=2 at extremes = artifact

LAA @ CLE.reasoning: Model correctly withheld pick pending LAA SP data. Live data now confirms Brent Suter (id=608718, veteran) starts for LAA vs Joey Cantillo for CLE. Model requires 100+ Statcast pitches from Suter before scoring SP-edge feature. Directional lean is CLE: CLE is 21-21 vs LAA 16-25; CLE lineup is strong (Kwan, J.Ramirez, Hoskins, Bazzana); Suter is a serviceable but not dominant starter. PENDING designation confirmed; no stake warranted until model processes Suter's quality score.
lessons_applied: PENDING_SP_DATA correctly applied: DET@KC 05-10, NYY@MIL 05-10 both validated this protocol
